Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • As Cease-Fire With Iran Wavers, US Expands Its Military Footprint RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- As its cease-fire with Iran falters, the United States is significantly expanding its military capabilities in the Middle East, giving Washington the option of escalating the two-month-long conflict.

  • Iran's Air Force conducted sorties to strike US bases in Iraq, Kuwait, Qatar early in war: Army Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- The Iranian Army's Air Force carried out multiple sorties against enemy bases in regional countries, including an anti-Iran group's base in Erbil, Iraq, as well as targets in Kuwait and Qatar, at the outset of the US-Israeli war, an Army spokesman said on Tuesday.
  • Post-war transit through Hormuz only possible under Iran's security protocols: Defense official Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- An Iranian deputy defense minister says the country would allow passage through the Strait of Hormuz in the Persian Gulf after the conclusion of the US-Israeli aggression under protocols that protect Iran's security.
  • Japanese tanker transits Strait of Hormuz after obtaining Iran's permission Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- A Japanese-owned supertanker carrying two million barrels of crude oil has successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz after securing permission from Iranian authorities, marking a rare passage through the strategic waterway since the launch of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

  • US Hits Iran's 'Shadow Banking' Network With New Sanctions RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- The US has announced new sanctions targeting 35 entities and individuals tied to Iran's covert financial network.
  • United States Cuts Off Billions in Illicit Funding to Iranian Regime US Dept. of State 28 Apr 2026 -- The United States exerts maximum pressure across every domain to stop the Iranian regime from threatening American interests and to hold it accountable.
  • Economic Fury Targets Iran Shadow Banking Facilitators US Treasury 28 Apr 2026 -- Today, as part of Economic Fury, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated 35 entities and individuals that oversee Iran's shadow banking architecture, facilitating the movement of the equivalent of tens of billions of dollars tied to sanctions evasion and Iran's sponsorship of terrorism. These networks allow Iran's armed forces—including the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)—to access the international financial system to receive payment for illicit oil sales, purchase sensitive components for missiles and other weapons systems, and transfer money to Iran's terrorist proxies.
  • Treasury Warns of Sanctions Risks Linked to China-Based Independent "Teapot" Oil Refineries US Treasury 28 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is alerting financial institutions to the sanctions risks associated with independent "teapot" oil refineries in China, primarily in Shandong Province, given their continued role in importing and refining Iranian crude oil.
  • Trump claims Iran wants US to open Strait of Hormuz 'as soon as possible' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 28 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Tuesday claimed that Iran is in a "state of collapse" and that Tehran has asked Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz "as soon as possible."
  • Trump attacks German chancellor over criticism of US aggression against Iran Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has lashed out at Friedrich Merz after the German chancellor criticized the American aggression against Iran for lacking a clear exit strategy and said Tehran was "humiliating" Washington with its negotiating tactics.
  • US Official: Trump Not Happy With Iran's Latest Proposal RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has expressed significant dissatisfaction with the latest proposal from Tehran, primarily because the offer fails to tackle the complexities of Iran's nuclear program, a senior US official confirmed to RFE/RL on April 27.

  • Iran's FM says U.S. continued "destructive habits" are slowing diplomatic progress People's Daily 28 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi on Monday blamed the slow progress of diplomacy between Iran and the United States on the latter's continuation of its "destructive habits."
  • April 28: Iran bolsters regional position with Strait of Hormuz control as US strategy falters Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Iran on Tuesday reinforced its position as the decisive power in West Asia, asserting full authority over the Strait of Hormuz, while the US position continued to falter.
  • IRGC Navy to confront any fresh American aggression with 'surprise tactics': Official Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy's deputy for political affairs says the naval force's "surprise tactics" await the United States in the event of any fresh miscalculation.
  • Iran has legal right to act in Hormuz, holds US responsible for disruptions: UN mission Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Iran, which is not a party to the 1982 UN Convention on the Law of the Sea, asserts its legal right to take "necessary and proportionate measures" in the Strait of Hormuz, and holds the United States responsible for any disruptions to maritime transport in the vital waterway, the country's permanent mission to the United Nations said.
  • US can no longer dictate its policies to independent nations: Iran Defense Ministry Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- The spokesperson for Iran's Ministry of Defense says the United States is no longer in a position to dictate its policies to independent nations, adding that this reality has been demonstrated to the world through the resilience of the Iranian people and armed forces.
  • Iran says EU's insistence on sanctions hastens its 'embarrassing descent into irrelevance' Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson says the EU's insistence on inhumane sanctions against Iran demonstrates Europe's double standards and hastens its "embarrassing descent into irrelevance."
  • Araghchi hails 'depth and strength of strategic partnership' between Iran and Russia Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi has praised the unbreakable strategic partnership between Iran and Russia, stressing that recent regional developments have only served to highlight the depth and resilience of Tehran-Moscow ties.
  • Iran: Responsibility for any disruption in Strait of Hormuz lies squarely with US aggression Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Iran has categorically stated that any disruption or obstruction in maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waters is the direct responsibility of the United States and its allies, whose reckless and illegal actions have turned a vital international waterway into a zone of heightened tension and danger.

  • From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks UN News 28 Apr 2026 -- Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to send shockwaves through global food systems, the UN Food and Agriculture Organization has warned, as a widening crisis across the Middle East continues to strain humanitarian operations and deepen instability.
  • Strait of Hormuz crisis: FAO Director-General outlines risks, actions and policy responses FAO 28 Apr 2026 -- QU Dongyu addresses FAO Council on implications for world food security and agriculture due to supply chain disruptions in the Gulf region

  • Illegal US-Israel military operations against Iran the root cause of obstruction in the Strait of Hormuz: Chinese envoy to the UN Global Times 28 Apr 2026 -- The root cause of the obstruction of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz is the US and Israel's illegal military operations against Iran, Fu Cong, China's permanent representative to the United Nations, said at a UN Security Council meeting on maritime safety on Monday local time.

  • Moscow, Tehran to support each other amid US aggression: Russia's defense minister Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Russia's minister of defense has expressed confidence that Moscow and Tehran would stand in support of each other amid the situation arising from the United States' unlawful aggression targeting the Islamic Republic.

  • Pakistani Mediators Expecting Second Iranian Offer to Settle US-Iran Conflict - Reports Sputnik 28 Apr 2026 -- Mediators in Pakistan are expecting to receive a second proposal from Iran to settle the conflict with the United States, CNN reported on Tuesday, citing sources close to the talks.

  • Iran: Statement by the Spokesperson on executions European External Action Service (EEAS) 28 Apr 2026 -- The current pace of executions in Iran is appalling. The EU has a firm and principled opposition to the use of capital punishment in all cases and in all circumstances. The death penalty is incompatible with the inalienable right to life and the absolute prohibition of cruel, inhuman or degrading punishment. It fails to act as deterrent to crime and makes miscarriages of justice irreversible. The EU urges Iranian authorities to pursue a consistent policy towards the abolition of the death penalty altogether.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul at the Open Debate on Maritime Security in the United Nations Security Council Germany Federal Foreign Office 28 Apr 2026
  • Attendance of State Minister for Foreign Affairs KUNIMITSU to UN Security Council High-Level Open Debate Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 28 Apr 2026

  • Iran, US Locked In Strategic 'Staring Contest' Over Hormuz, Says Former CENTCOM Official Joe Buccino RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- The US Navy's blockade of Iranian ports, now in its third week, has stopped almost all of the country's maritime trade, including key oil exports. But it has also ratcheted up tensions between the two sides as they hold sporadic peace negotiations.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Multinational Forces Validate Defensive Readiness During Exercise Balikatan 2026 DoD News 28 Apr 2026 -- Over 500 service members from the United States, Philippines, Australia and New Zealand successfully coordinated fires from land and air to defeat a simulated adversary attack as part of Exercise Balikatan 2026, April 27.
  • From Pier to Platform: Seabees Enable Autonomous Operations During Obangame Express 2026 US Navy 28 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Navy Seabees assigned to Naval Mobile Construction Battalion (NMCB) 1 and 22nd Naval Construction Regiment (NCR) are enabling the next generation of maritime operations during Exercise Obangame Express (OE) 2026, constructing expeditionary infrastructure designed to support autonomous systems in the Gulf of Guinea.
  • USS Dwight D. Eisenhower completes Planned Incremental Availability at Norfolk Naval Shipyard US Navy Press Release 28 Apr 2026 -- USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (CVN 69) has completed sea trials, marking the successful early completion of its Planned Incremental Availability (PIA) at Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY).
  • DAF launches plan to bolster AI workforce United States Space Force 28 Apr 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force approved a comprehensive strategy this month to aggressively recruit, train and retain top-tier artificial intelligence professionals, aiming to operationalize AI and maintain a decisive competitive advantage in an era of rapid technological change.
  • Historic Cape Canaveral Space Force Station Skid Strip renovated for modern space mission needs United States Space Force 28 Apr 2026 -- Leaders reopened the historic runway known as the Skid Strip this week after a two-year, large-scale renovation, marking a new era for one of the most critical pieces of infrastructure supporting U.S. space launch capabilities.
  • Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson, Commander of U.S. Africa Command Visits Algeria to Strengthen Strategic Cooperation on Regional Security AFRICOM 28 Apr 2026 -- Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson, Commander of United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) undertook an official visit to Algeria, Apr 27-28, 2026, aimed at reinforcing bilateral cooperation and advancing shared efforts toward regional stability and peace.

  • Senior national officials for resilience meet at NATO to address the crucial role of resilience in Allied security NATO 28 Apr 2026 -- On Tuesday (28 April 2026), senior national officials for resilience met at the NATO headquarters to discuss progress in boosting resilience-related investments. Experts from all NATO Allies exchanged views and knowledge with the aim of better protecting our societies. To prepare the Alliance for future risks and threats, close cooperation between NATO, national militaries, and civilian authorities is essential. This cooperation helps strengthen resilience across all sectors.
  • NATO logistics directors meet to further strengthen logistics readiness NATO 28 Apr 2026 -- On Tuesday (28 April 2026), NATO's Logistics Committee met at NATO Headquarters in Brussels to review the progress made in strengthening the Alliance's logistics for collective defence. Discussions focused on preparing and exercising reinforcement, Logistics Action Plan Implementation, delivering the necessary Host Nation Support, and ensuring effective logistics in a contested environment. Allies stressed that practising these logistics efforts is essential. Exercises help ensure that everything works, not just on paper, and improve overall readiness.

  • Russia Hoped Africa Corps Would Replicate Wagner's Success. It's Not Going Well. RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- Hundreds of masked Russian soldiers rumbled out of the northern Mali city of Kidal over the weekend, leaving in their wake huge caches of weaponry, equipment, and a downed helicopter -- not to mention major questions for the future of the Russian Defense Ministry mercenary group known as Africa Corps.
  • Andrei Belousov outlines promising areas for cooperation within CSO Russia MoD 28 Apr 2026 -- Addressing to the meeting of Ministers of Defence of the SCO member countries, Andrei Belousov outlined promising areas for the Organisation countries cooperation.
  • Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov outlines main hotbeds of regional tension in SCO area Russia MoD 28 Apr 2026 -- 'In order to maintain global dominance, the United States and the collective West are destroying the foundations of the global security architecture. Their aggressive course exacerbates geopolitical divisions, undermines strategic stability and key peace agreements,' said Andrei Belousov.
  • Head of Russian Defence Ministry takes part in meeting of defence ministers of SCO states Russia MoD 28 Apr 2026 -- Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov took part in the meeting of heads of military departments of Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) member countries held in Bishkek on Tuesday.
  • Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov holds talks with Minister of Defence of Tajikistan in Bishkek Russia MoD 28 Apr 2026 -- The Russia-Tajikistan cooperation in the military sphere has a great potential, said Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov.
  • Statement by Russian Defence Ministry Russia MoD 28 Apr 2026 -- On 9 May 2026, a military parade will take place in Moscow to mark the 81st Anniversary of the Victory in the Great Patriotic War.

  • Decisions Have Been Made to Secure Sufficient Financial Resources for Our State and Our Defense - Address by the President President of Ukraine 28 Apr 2026
  • More drones for the front: DOT-Chain Defence introduces an advance payment mechanism to accelerate deliveries to the Armed Forces of Ukraine Ukraine MOD 28 Apr 2026 -- At the initiative of the Ministry of Defence, the Defence Procurement Agency DOT is launching an advance payment mechanism for manufacturers on the DOT-Chain Defence weapons marketplace: effective drone manufacturers will now receive advance payments. The goal is to provide the market with working capital to accelerate production and delivery of drones to combat units.
  • Ukraine can now reach targets in russia up to 1,750 km away, expanding deep strike range by 2.5 times Ukraine MOD 28 Apr 2026 -- Since the onset of the full-scale invasion, the Defence Forces of Ukraine have increased the range of deep strike operations into the aggressor's deep rear areas by over 2.5 times. Whereas in 2022 a military target was struck at a range of approximately 630 km, Ukraine's long-range strike capabilities can now successfully strike the enemy's war machine at a distance of around 1,750 km.
  • Brave1 tests a new type of FPV drones in field trials Ukraine MOD 28 Apr 2026 -- The Brave1 defense innovation cluster has conducted field testing of a new generation of FPV drones. Efforts are underway to accelerate the delivery of these UAVs to the Defenсe Forces of Ukraine. Some of the tested models have already demonstrated high effectiveness in challenging conditions.

  • Minister Wellington Koo Li-hsiung Inspects Training Units at Northern Combined Training Center - Commends Effectiveness of Realistic Combat Training ROC MND 28 Apr 2026
  • Defense budget amount contingent on U.S. LOA: KMT chair CNA 28 Apr 2026 -- Opposition Kuomintang (KMT) Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) said Tuesday that a proposed special defense budget should be approved only after Taiwan receives a formal Letter of Offer and Acceptance (LOA) from the United States.

  • Air Force flies Bersama Shield Australia DOD 28 Apr 2026 -- A Royal Australian Air Force C-27J Spartan aircraft and 35 Squadron pilots and crew are providing air transport and support tasks as well as conducting training missions, as part of a 130-person Australian Defence Force contingent participating in Exercise Bersama Shield.
  • The communications backbone of the battlespace Australia DOD 28 Apr 2026 -- Army signals personnel deployed across the country for Exercise Mercury Run to prepare 1st (Australian) Division for the year's major warfighting exercises.
  • Albanese Government strengthens Army's long-range strike capability Australia DOD 28 Apr 2026 -- In a major enhancement to the Australian Army's long-range strike capability, the Albanese Government has selected the High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) and the Precision Strike Missile (PrSM), investing $2.3 billion over the decade and delivering a second long‑range fires regiment at the Edinburgh Defence Precinct in South Australia.
  • Pacific Heads of Security advancing collective action Australia DOD 28 Apr 2026 -- The heads of police, military, customs and immigration agencies from across 24 jurisdictions will convene in Brisbane this week at the eighth annual Joint Heads of Pacific Security (JHoPS) meeting to discuss strategic priorities and advance Pacific-led responses to regional security challenges.

  • UN sanctions monitoring patrols observe vessels of interest, possible ship-to-ship transfer New Zealand Defence Force 28 Apr 2026 -- The New Zealand Defence Force (NZDF) has concluded a series of maritime patrols over international waters in the Yellow Sea and East China Sea, monitoring North Korean evasion of United Nations Security Council (UNSC) sanctions.

  • Raksha Mantri holds bilateral meetings with his Kyrgyz, Kazakh & Belarusian counterparts in Bishkek to further strengthen defence cooperation India PIB 28 Apr 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh held bilateral meetings with his Kyrgyz, Kazakh and Belarusian counterparts on the sidelines of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers' Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan on April 28, 2026. During the meeting with the Defence Minister of Kyrgyzstan Major General Mukambetov Ruslan Mustafaevich, both sides discussed ways to strengthen the existing bilateral defence cooperation.
  • Op Sindoor demonstrated India's firm resolve that terrorism epicentres are no longer immune to justifiable punishment: Raksha Mantri at SCO Defence Ministers' Meeting in Kyrgyzstan India PIB 28 Apr 2026 -- "Operation Sindoor demonstrated India's firm resolve that terrorism epicentres are no longer immune to justifiable punishment," said Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh as he addressed the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers' Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan on April 28, 2026, underlining the need to forge a unified front to tackle the "evils" of terrorism, separatism and extremism by eliminating safe havens and rejecting any political exceptions. He asserted that the sight of state-sponsored cross-border terrorism which attacks the sovereignty of a nation must not be lost as there is no place for double standards.
  • INDIAN OCEAN SHIP SAGAR ARRIVES AT CHANGI NAVAL BASE, SINGAPORE India PIB 28 Apr 2026 -- Indian Ocean Ship (IOS) SAGAR, INS Sunayna, arrived at Changi Naval Base, Singapore, on 26 Apr 2026, marking her fourth port call during the ongoing IOS SAGAR deployment under the vision of MAHASAGAR (Mutual and Holistic Advancement for Security and Growth Across Regions).

  • Takaichi clamors for sustainable combat capacity for protracted warfare by hyping security environment in 1st expert panel meeting on security documents revision Global Times 28 Apr 2026 -- The Japanese government held the first meeting of its panel of experts at the Prime Minister's Official Residence on Monday as part of efforts to push for revising the three [national security] documents during which Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi claimed that Japan must secure sustainable combat capabilities and prepare for protracted warfare, according to Japanese media reports.

  • Pakistani Navy's Hangor-Class Submarines: A New Era in Undersea Warfare Sputnik 28 Apr 2026 -- The third-generation vessels are based on the Chinese Type 039A, and will replace ageing French Agosta 90B-class submarines.

  • Azerbaijani Defense Minister pays official visit to Italy Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 28 Apr 2026 -- A delegation led by Azerbaijan's Minister of Defense, Colonel General Zakir Hasanov, is on an official visit to the Italian Republic, the Ministry told AZERTAC.

  • Global military spending rise continues as European and Asian expenditures surge Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 28 Apr 2026 -- World military expenditure reached $2887 billion in 2025, an increase of 2.9 per cent in real terms over 2024, according to Stockholm International Peace Research Institute.

  • The President in Dubrovnik: The Three Seas Initiative strengthens transatlantic cooperation, security, and economic growth President of the Republic of Lithuania 28 Apr 2026 -- On Tuesday, President Gitanas Nausėda took part in the Three Seas Initiative Summit in Dubrovnik, dedicated to issues of regional security, resilience, and economic development.

  • Minister Rantanen: The Government will strengthen drone defence Finnish Government 28 Apr 2026 -- The upcoming supplementary budget will significantly improve the counter-drone capabilities of the Border Guard and the police. The Government agreed on this in its spending limits session last week. Discussions will continue in the supplementary budget session at the end of May.

  • 500 Balikatan soldiers hold counter-landing drills in Palawan Philippine News Agency 28 Apr 2026 -- About 500 personnel from the Philippines, the United States, Australia, and New Zealand militaries, taking part in this year's "Balikatan" maneuvers, conducted a counter-landing exercise in Aporawan, Palawan on Monday.

  • Joint Statement in Support of Panama's Sovereignty Between the United States of America, Bolivia, Costa Rica, Guyana, Paraguay, and Trinidad and Tobago US Dept. of State 28 Apr 2026 -- The text of the following statement was released by the Governments of the United States of America, Bolivia, Costa Rica, Guyana, Paraguay, and Trinidad and Tobago.

  • Contracts for April 28, 2026 DoD
  • Google employees urge CEO to reject Pentagon AI use in classified work Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 28 Apr 2026 -- Hundreds of employees at Google have urged CEO Sundar Pichai not to allow the company's artificial intelligence tools to be used by the Pentagon in classified settings, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • PM Netanyahu: "Today, we blew up a massive Hezbollah terrorist tunnel." Israel - Prime Minister 28 Apr 2026
  • Israeli military issues fresh displacement orders for south Lebanon beyond 'yellow line' Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Israel's military has issued new displacement orders for residents of more than a dozen villages and towns in southern Lebanon amid widespread violations of a ceasefire agreement by the occupying regime.
  • Israel exploiting access to drinking water as 'weapon' in war-ravaged Gaza: MSF Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- An international and independent medical charity says Israeli authorities are systematically depriving the Palestinian population in the war-ravaged Gaza Strip of the water they need to survive, condemning a campaign of "collective punishment" against residents of the coastal region.

  • Israel exploiting access to drinking water as 'weapon' in war-ravaged Gaza: MSF Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- An international and independent medical charity says Israeli authorities are systematically depriving the Palestinian population in the war-ravaged Gaza Strip of the water they need to survive, condemning a campaign of "collective punishment" against residents of the coastal region.

  • Hezbollah's Deadly Fiberoptic Tethered Drones Poke Huge Hole in Israel's Defenses Sputnik 28 Apr 2026 -- The Lebanese militia's new UAVs have taken its asymmetric warfare capabilities to new heights.$400-500 UAVs built from 3D-printed plastic parts, Soviet RPG grenades and cheap FPV controls linked to spools of commercially-sourced fiberoptic cable are taking on Israeli tanks worth millions.

  • Amid Tenuous Ceasefire, Soaring Humanitarian Needs, Security Council Hears Updates on Transitional Governance Structure, Reconstruction Efforts in Gaza UN Security Council 28 Apr 2026 -- Amid a tenuous ceasefire in Gaza, punctuated by airstrikes and shelling, as well as soaring humanitarian needs, the Security Council today heard updates on what a transitional governance structure and reconstruction efforts would resemble in the devastated Strip during the organ's quarterly open debate on the Palestinian question.

  • EU Statement - UN Security Council: Open debate on the situation in the Middle East, including the Palestinian question European External Action Service (EEAS) 28 Apr 2026
  • Palestine - Local elections in the West Bank and Deir al-Balah France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 28 Apr 2026
  • Briefing to the Security Council on AHLC Government of Norway 28 Apr 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the Ad Hoc Liason Committee on Palestine by Minister of Foreign Affairs Espen Barth Eide.
  • The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians Issues Its 126th Report, Documenting 824 Palestinians Killed and 2,318 Wounded in Gaza Since the Ceasefire Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 28 Apr 2026 -- Israeli occupation forces have continued their bombardment of the Gaza Strip, killing 824 Palestinians and wounding 2,318 since the ceasefire agreement took effect
  • We are appalled by the deteriorating trajectory in the West Bank and East Jerusalem: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 28 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on the Middle East.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Somalia, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Afghanistan, Sudan UNOCHA 28 Apr 2026 -- UN relief chief visits as hunger surges, funding falls short

  • CCG expels Japanese vessel illegally intruding into China's territorial waters of Diaoyu Dao, says newly appointed spokesperson Global Times 28 Apr 2026 -- Japanese fishing boat Tsurumaru intruded into the territorial waters of China's Diaoyu Dao on Tuesday. China Coast Guard (CCG) vessels took necessary measures to warn and expel it in accordance with the law, Jiang Lue, spokesperson of the CCG, stated on Tuesday, according to a statement released via CCG's official WeChat account.
  • China expels Japanese boat intruding into Diaoyu Dao territorial waters PRC MND 28 Apr 2026 -- China Coast Guard (CCG) on Tuesday took necessary measures to warn and expel Japanese fishing boat Tsurumaru, which intruded into the territorial waters of China's Diaoyu Dao, said CCG spokesperson Jiang Lue.
  • China's envoy rebukes Japan's remarks on East and South China Seas at UN meeting on the spot, saying they 'confound black and white' Global Times 28 Apr 2026 -- Chinese envoy denounced remarks by Japan over the South China Sea and East China Sea as "completely confound black and white" at a UN Security Council meeting on ‌Monday and accused Tokyo of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Straits.

  • PLA conducts routine patrol in South China Sea, slams Philippines' so-called 'joint patrols' as disrupting regional stability Global Times 28 Apr 2026 -- The navy of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Southern Theater Command (STC) conducted a routine patrol in the South China Sea from Tuesday, Senior Captain Zhai Shichen, spokesperson for the PLA Southern Theater Command, announced on Tuesday.
  • Chinese PLA Navy conducts routine patrols in South China Sea PRC MND 28 Apr 2026 -- The navy troops assigned to Chinese PLA Southern Theater Command (STC) conducted routine patrols in the South China Sea on April 28, said Senior Captain Zhai ShiChen, spokesperson for the Chinese PLA STC.

  • Human rights update on Syria OHCHR 28 Apr 2026 -- Our colleagues in Syria have just concluded a visit to Al-Hassakeh Governorate in the northeast of the country, where they received firsthand testimonies and accounts of past and present human rights violations and abuses. These include reports of significant numbers of killings, enforced disappearances, arbitrary detention, and deportation of detainees.

  • Myanmar: UN expert calls for international action to end impunity and break cycle of violence OHCHR 28 Apr 2026 -- A UN expert today called on the international community to step up efforts for accountability in Myanmar, saying that decades of impunity have led the country to a human rights catastrophe.

  • Minister Anand and Moroccan counterpart discuss Canada's position on Western Sahara Canada 28 Apr 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, spoke with her Moroccan counterpart, Nasser Bourita, Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates.

  • Statement by Russian Defence Ministry Russia MoD 28 Apr 2026 -- On 25 April 2026, at around 05:30 in the Republic of Mali, illegal armed groups of theJama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin groups (JNIM) and the Azawad Liberation Front's groups (ALF), under the general guidance and coordination of actions, attempted an armed coup d'état.
  • Russia's African Corps Foils Militant Coup Attempt in Mali - MoD Sputnik 28 Apr 2026 -- Illegal armed groups in Mali attempted an armed coup on April 25, the Russian Defense Ministry said on Tuesday.
  • Press Release : India strongly condemns terrorist attacks in Mali India MEA 28 Apr 2026

  • Sudan's RSF leaders build Dubai property empire with UAE backing: Investigative group Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Leaders of Sudan's so-called Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and their network have amassed millions in luxury assets in Dubai, an investigation reveals, as the United Arab Emirates (UAE) is accused of helping a financial lifeline for a militant group that committed genocide in the crisis-hit African country.

  • "Children in Darfur have reached a breaking point" UNICEF 28 Apr 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by UNICEF Sudan Representative Sheldon Yett - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva
  • Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence' UN News 28 Apr 2026 -- Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement - but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

  • Over half of South Sudan's population faces acute hunger crisis UN News 28 Apr 2026 -- Conflict and displacement are intensifying South Sudan's hunger crisis, with 7.8 million people facing high levels of acute food insecurity while 2.2 million children are suffering from acute malnutrition, according to a joint statement on Tuesday from UN agencies.
  • Hunger intensifies in South Sudan as 7.8 million people face high acute food insecurity and 2.2 million children suffer acute malnutrition UNICEF 28 Apr 2026 -- The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), the World Food Programme (WFP) and UNICEF today warned that a deepening hunger crisis in South Sudan is pushing 7.8 million people into high levels of acute food insecurity (IPC Phase 3 or above) between April and July 2026, according to the latest Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) analysis. This represents 56 percent of the population—one of the highest levels of acute food insecurity in the world today.

  • Army calls on Reds to free Fil-Am Philippine News Agency 28 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Army's 2nd Infantry Division (ID) on Tuesday called on the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People's Army (CPP-NPA) to release a Filipino-American currently in their custody, so she could reunite with her family in the United States.
  • Reds violating int'l, local laws to recruit minors Philippine News Agency 28 Apr 2026 -- The Communist Party of the Philippines-New People's Army-National Democratic Front (CPP-NPA-NDF) is violating international and local laws for its continued recruitment and use of minors in its ongoing campaign against the Philippine government, a ranking National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict (NTF-ELCAC) official said Monday night.

News Reports

  • Federal Grand Jury Indicts Former FBI Director James Comey for Threats to Harm President Trump US Dept. of Justice 28 Apr 2026 -- A federal grand jury in the Eastern District of North Carolina today returned an indictment charging former FBI director James Comey with making threats to harm President Donald J. Trump.

  • SSU and National Police detain russia's agents plotting assassination attempt on Hero of Ukraine Security Service of Ukraine 28 Apr 2026

  • West losing leadership position to Global South: Russia's president Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin says the West is losing its economic and political leadership position in the world, giving way to the countries of the Global South.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on April 28, 2026 PRC MofA 28 Apr 2026
  • Beijing Pushes Back As US Sanctions Chinese Oil Refinery Over Iran Links RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- Beijing has pushed back on a US decision to sanction the Hengli oil refinery, rejecting Washington's accusations that the company "plays an outsized role" in buying Iranian oil.

  • Taiwan FM returns from Eswatini after Lai's canceled trip CNA 28 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung (林佳龍) returned to Taiwan early Tuesday after a brief visit to Eswatini as special envoy for President Lai Ching-te (賴清德), following the last-minute cancellation of Lai's planned trip to the African ally to attend celebrations for its king, reportedly due to Chinese pressure.
  • Chinese warships sail near Penghu CNA 28 Apr 2026 -- Two Chinese naval vessels sailed into waters southwest of Taiwan's Penghu Islands on Monday, with no official explanation from Beijing.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 28 Apr 2026

  • Satellite imagery reveals increased activity at North Korean nuclear complex Radio Free Asia 28 Apr 2026 -- At North Korea's Yongbyon Nuclear Complex, heightened activity observed on satellite imagery indicates that Pyongyang is increasing its capacity to produce nuclear weapons, experts told Radio Free Asia.
  • North Korea Is Honoring Some Of Its Soldiers Killed In The Ukraine War. But What About The Rest? RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026 -- North Korea had earlier commemorated just 101 soldiersfallen in the military campaign in western Russia. Neither Pyongyang nor Moscow have released casualty figures from the Kursk campaign.

  • President pledges firm pursuit of peaceful coexistence with NK KOREA.net 28 Apr 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on April 27 said, "My administration will steadfastly pursue a policy of peaceful coexistence on the Korean Peninsula."
  • Sentence for ex-S. Korean president Yoon's wife increased to 4 years on appeal for corruption People's Daily 28 Apr 2026 -- The sentence for Kim Keon-hee, wife of South Korea's former President Yoon Suk-yeol, was increased to four years on appeal for corruption, live footage showed Tuesday.

  • IRGC dismantles four-member terror cell in western Iran Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has dealt a major blow to mercenary and traitorous elements of separatist terrorist groups in Kordestan province, confiscating weapons, combat equipment and satellite communication devices, authorities announced on Tuesday.

  • Iraq - Appointment of Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 28 Apr 2026
  • Iraqi president names banker Ali al-Zaidi as prime minister-designate Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Iraq's newly-elected President Nizar Amedi has named Ali al-Zaidi as the country's prime minister-designate and officially tasked him with forming a government.

  • Netanyahu attends 81st court session to answer corruption charges Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu appeared Tuesday before the District Court in Tel Aviv for the 81st time since his trial began in 2020, responding to corruption charges against him.
  • Netanyahu attends 81st court session to answer corruption charges Press TV 28 Apr 2026 -- Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu appeared Tuesday before the District Court in Tel Aviv for the 81st time since his trial began in 2020, responding to corruption charges against him.
  • Mossad Director David Barnea at the 2025 Commendation Ceremony Israel - Prime Minister 28 Apr 2026 -- Mossad Director David Barnea at the 2025 Commendation Ceremony: "We acquired strategic and tactical intelligence from the heart of the enemy's secrets and demonstrated new, groundbreaking operational capabilities in target countries. We implemented a clandestine diplomatic campaign to forge regional alliances."

  • The Daily Press Briefing (04/28/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 28 Apr 2026

  • Opening remarks by Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul at the reception at the German House in support of the candidacy for a non-permanent seat on the United Nations Security Council 2027-2028 Germany Federal Foreign Office 28 Apr 2026

  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 28 Apr 2026

  • Japan-Egypt Summit Telephone Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 28 Apr 2026
  • Japan-Australia Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 28 Apr 2026

  • HRH the Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session in Jeddah SPA 28 Apr 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, chaired the Cabinet session held today in Jeddah

  • United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 28 Apr 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is quitting the Opec and Opec+ groups of major oil producing nations next month after nearly 60 years of membership, according to BBC.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Who Escaped The EU's Latest Sanctions, And Brussels' Security Guarantees For Ukraine RFE/RL 28 Apr 2026

  • ASEAN: Opening remarks by High Representative/Vice-President Kaja Kallas at the 25th EU-ASEAN Ministerial Meeting European External Action Service (EEAS) 28 Apr 2026
  • 25th ASEAN-EU Ministerial Meeting : Press remarks by High Representative / Vice-President Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 28 Apr 2026

  • World News in Brief: Syria human rights update, Cuba post-hurricane support still vital, impunity and violence in Myanmar UN News 28 Apr 2026 -- Three mass graves were recently uncovered in northeastern Syria, including one reportedly at the site of a former detention centre run by the Kurdish-backed Syrian Armed Forces (SDF).
